metadata.title: ICH Safety Reporting Rules — Comprehensive Extraction

adverse_event.definition: Any untoward medical occurrence in a patient or clinical investigation subject administered a pharmaceutical product and which does not necessarily have a causal relationship with this treatment. An AE can be any unfavourable and unintended sign (including an abnormal laboratory finding), symptom, or disease temporally associated with the use of a medicinal (investigational) product, whether or not related to the product.

adverse_drug_reaction_preapproval.definition: All noxious and unintended responses to a medicinal product related to any dose. A causal relationship is at least a reasonable possibility (i.e., the relationship cannot be ruled out).

adverse_drug_reaction_postmarket.definition: A response to a drug which is noxious and unintended and which occurs at doses normally used in man for prophylaxis, diagnosis, or therapy of diseases or for modification of physiological function (WHO definition). All spontaneous reports are presumed to have a causal relationship.

unexpected_adr.definition: An adverse reaction, the nature or severity of which is not consistent with the applicable product information (e.g., Investigator's Brochure for unapproved products or package insert/SPC for approved products). Includes class effects not specifically mentioned in product information. Reports adding clinically meaningful information on a known, serious ADR also qualify.

serious_adverse_event.definition: Any untoward medical occurrence that at any dose meets one or more seriousness criteria.
criteria: Is life-threatening (patient at risk of death at the time of the event)
Requires inpatient hospitalisation or prolongation of existing hospitalisation
Results in persistent or significant disability/incapacity
Is an important medical event that may jeopardise the patient or require intervention to prevent one of the above outcomes

signal.definition: Information from one or multiple sources (including observations and experiments) that suggests a new potentially causal association, or a new aspect of a known association, between an intervention and an event or set of related events, that is judged to be of sufficient likelihood to justify further action to verify.

identified_risk.definition: An untoward occurrence with adequate evidence of association with the medicinal product.

potential_risk.definition: An untoward occurrence with some basis for suspicion of association but not confirmed. Examples: non-clinical findings not observed clinically, AEs where magnitude does not suggest causality, known class effects.

important_missing_information.definition: Gaps in knowledge regarding the safety profile, including populations not studied or inadequately studied, long-term safety in chronic use, and special populations exposed in clinical practice but not in trials.

spontaneous_report.definition: Unsolicited communication by an HCP or consumer to a company, regulatory authority, or other organisation describing one or more ADRs in a patient given one or more medicinal products, not deriving from a study or organized data collection scheme.

stimulated_report.definition: Reports arising from Dear Healthcare Professional letters, publication in the press, or questioning of HCPs by company representatives. Classified as spontaneous.

solicited_report.definition: Reports derived from organised data collection systems (clinical trials, registries, PSPs, MRPs, surveys). NOT considered spontaneous. Classified as study reports requiring causality assessment.

other_observations.definition: Exposure with pregnancy/breastfeeding; lack of efficacy/effect; overdose, abuse, misuse, medication error, occupational exposure; off-label use. May occur with or without associated AEs/ADRs.

organised_data_collection_system.definition: An activity that gathers data relevant to an MAH's product or medical disease area in a planned manner. Includes clinical trials, non-interventional studies, PSPs, MRPs, and social listening/sentiment analysis.

digital_platform.definition: Software and technology enabling transmission of information between users — includes social media, websites, internet forums, chat rooms, mobile health technologies, and apps.

severity_vs_seriousness.definition: Severity describes intensity of a specific event (mild, moderate, severe). Seriousness is defined by regulatory reporting obligations. A severe AE is not necessarily serious (e.g., severe headache).

international_birth_date.definition: Date of the first marketing authorisation for any product containing the active substance granted to any company in any country in the world. Anchors PBRER reporting periodicity.

development_international_birth_date.definition: Date of the sponsor's first authorisation to conduct a clinical trial in any country worldwide. Determines DSUR annual reporting period.

data_lock_point.definition: The date that defines the cut-off for data to be included in the periodic report. All data available up to and including the DLP should be incorporated.

reference_safety_information.definition: For PBRER: the Company Core Safety Information (CCSI) within the Company Core Data Sheet (CCDS). For DSUR: the Investigator's Brochure (IB) in effect at the start of the reporting period. Serves as the benchmark for determining expectedness.

adverse_event_of_special_interest.definition: A serious or non-serious event of scientific/medical concern specific to the sponsor's product or programme; warrants ongoing monitoring and rapid communication.

non_serious.definition: Does not meet any seriousness criterion

by_severity.note: Severity is distinct from seriousness and does not drive regulatory reporting

by_expectedness.expected: Nature, severity, specificity, or outcome IS consistent with Reference Safety Information
by_expectedness.unexpected: Nature, severity, specificity, or outcome is NOT consistent with Reference Safety Information. A known ADR with fatal outcome is unexpected unless labelling specifically states the ADR might be associated with a fatal outcome.

by_causality.related: Causal relationship is at least a reasonable possibility (cannot be ruled out)
by_causality.spontaneous_presumption: All spontaneous reports are presumed to have a causal relationship

by_source.spontaneous: Unsolicited, not from organized data collection
by_source.stimulated: From Dear HCP letters, press publications — treated as spontaneous
by_source.solicited: From ODCS (trials, registries, PSPs, MRPs) — treated as study reports

serious_unexpected_adr.day_zero: Date any MAH personnel (including third parties) first obtains sufficient information to determine case meets minimum criteria

followup_reports.timeline: 15 calendar days — time clock restarts when additional medically relevant information is received

reclassification.rule: Day 0 = date of receipt of follow-up triggering reclassification (e.g., non-serious to serious)

required_elements: An identifiable reporter (name, initials, address, organisation, email, phone, or qualification)
An identifiable patient (age, age category, sex, initials, DOB, name, or patient ID)
At least one adverse event/reaction or other observation
At least one suspect or interacting medicinal product

followup_priority.special: Cases of special interest, cases that might lead to labelling changes
followup_priority.pregnancy: All pregnancy reports where embryo/foetus could have been exposed (includes paternal exposure per R1)

other_observation_reporting.lack_of_efficacy: ICSR only if required by regional requirements; especially for life-threatening diseases, vaccines, contraceptives
other_observation_reporting.overdose_abuse_misuse_med_error: ICSR only if required by regional requirements when no associated AE/ADR; includes near-miss medication errors
other_observation_reporting.pregnancy_breastfeeding_exposure: ICSR only if required by regional requirements when no AE/ADR; paternal exposure explicitly included
other_observation_reporting.off_label_use: ICSR only if required by regional requirements when no associated AE/ADR

important_safety_findings.definition: Safety findings not qualifying for ICSR reporting but that may change the benefit-risk balance
important_safety_findings.examples: Evidence of mutagenicity, teratogenicity, carcinogenicity, immunogenicity, increased mortality
important_safety_findings.timeline: Communicated to authorities as soon as possible

literature_screening.frequency: At least every two weeks (per local requirements)
literature_screening.scope: Worldwide scientific literature including published abstracts
literature_screening.rule: Each case in a publication is a separate report; expedited if serious + unexpected

digital_platform_screening.mah_platforms: Must regularly screen platforms under MAH responsibility
digital_platform_screening.external_platforms: NOT expected to screen external platforms, but if MAH conducts planned ODCS activity, must manage identified AEs/ADRs; incidental awareness triggers review obligation

icsr_data_elements_e2b_r3.format: XML based on ISO/HL7 27953-2:2011, UTF-8 encoding
icsr_data_elements_e2b_r3.transmission: ESTRI Gateway with public/private key encryption

batch_wrapper_N1.N.1.1: Type of Messages in Batch (Required, value: 1=ichicsr)
batch_wrapper_N1.N.1.5: Date of Batch Transmission (Required, Date/Time to second precision)

case_identification_C1.C.1.2: Date of Creation (Required, Date/Time to second)
case_identification_C1.C.1.3: Type of Report (Required: 1=Spontaneous, 2=Report from study, 3=Other, 4=Not available)
case_identification_C1.C.1.4: Date Report First Received from Source (Required, Date min day precision)
case_identification_C1.C.1.5: Date of Most Recent Information (Required, Date min day precision)
case_identification_C1.C.1.7: Fulfils Local Criteria for Expedited Report (Required, Boolean/NI)
case_identification_C1.C.1.9.1: Other Case Identifiers in Previous Transmissions (Required, Boolean/NI)
case_identification_C1.C.1.11.2: Reason for Nullification/Amendment (Required when C.1.11.1 populated)

primary_source_C2.C.2.r.1: Reporter Name fields (Optional: title, given, middle, family)
primary_source_C2.C.2.r.5: Primary Source for Regulatory Purposes (Required, Boolean)

patient_D.D.2: Age Information (DOB, age at onset, gestation period, age group)
patient_D.D.9: Death Information (date, cause, autopsy, MedDRA coded)

reactions_E.E.i.3.2a-f: Seriousness Criteria at Event Level (Required, 6 Boolean fields: death, life-threatening, hospitalisation, disability, congenital anomaly, other medically important)

tests_F.F.r.2: Test Name — free text and MedDRA coded (Optional)
tests_F.F.r.3: Test Result — code (1=Positive, 2=Negative, 3=Borderline, 4=Inconclusive) + value + free text

drugs_G.G.k.2: Drug Identification (proprietary name, MPID, nonproprietary name, PhPID, substance/specified substance, strength)
drugs_G.G.k.4.r: Dosage Information (dose, frequency, dates, duration, batch/lot, dosage text, dose form, route — repeatable)
drugs_G.G.k.8: Action Taken (1=Withdrawn, 2=Dose reduced, 3=Dose increased, 4=Dose not changed, 5=Unknown, 6=N/A)

controlled_vocabularies: MedDRA (OID: 2.16.840.1.113883.6.163) — AEs, indications, medical history, causes of death, test names, diagnoses
ISO IDMP (11238 Substances, 11239 Dose Forms & Routes, 11240 Units, 11615 MPID, 11616 PhPID)

nullification_amendment_rules.nullification: Used when entire case is void. NEW C.1.1 and C.1.8.1 required for resubmission. Reason required.
nullification_amendment_rules.amendment: Used for corrections after internal review. SAME C.1.1 and C.1.8.1 as original. C.1.5 NOT changed if no new source information. Reason required.

e2b_r3_study_type_values.2: Individual patient use (named patient/compassionate use/expanded access)
e2b_r3_study_type_values.6: ODCS with source data from a digital platform (new in R1)

data_presentation_rules.clinical_trial_exposure: Cumulative by treatment, age/sex, racial group
data_presentation_rules.marketing_exposure: Cumulative AND interval by indication, sex, age, dose, formulation, region
data_presentation_rules.post_marketing_ADRs: Interval AND cumulative counts: serious spontaneous, non-serious spontaneous, total spontaneous, serious solicited/non-interventional

line_listing_fields: Sponsor's adverse reaction case reference number
Date/time of onset of most serious adverse reaction
Dates of treatment and/or treatment duration

periodicity.submission_deadline: Within 60 calendar days after data lock point
periodicity.duration: As long as required by national/regional law
periodicity.rsi_source: Investigator's Brochure in effect at start of reporting period

standard_appendices: Cumulative Table of Important Regulatory Requests
Status of Ongoing and Completed Clinical Trials
Cumulative Summary Tabulations of Demographic Data
Line Listings of Serious Adverse Reactions
Cumulative Summary Tabulation of Serious Adverse Events
regional_appendices_US_IND: R1: Cumulative summary tabulation of SARs
R2: List of subjects who died
R3: List of subjects who dropped out with AEs
R4: Significant Phase I protocol modifications
R6: General investigation plan for coming year

signal_types.newly_identified: First identified during the reporting interval, prompting further actions or evaluation. Includes previously closed signals with new information.

signal_overview_table_fields: Reason for evaluation and summary of key data

dsur_signal_context.approach: Annual re-evaluation of identified and potential risks

three_pillars.important_identified_risks: Adverse reactions with adequate evidence of association
three_pillars.important_potential_risks: Basis for suspicion but not confirmed

active_surveillance: Drug event monitoring / Prescription Event Monitoring
additional.non_clinical_studies: Additional preclinical studies to address safety concerns

regulatory_concern_levels.below_5ms: No increased risk detected or too small to detect
regulatory_concern_levels.5_to_20ms: Inconclusive; some compounds associated with proarrhythmic risk

qtc_thresholds.negative_tqt_definition: Upper bound of one-sided 95% CI for largest time-matched mean effect on QTc excludes 10 ms (equivalent: upper bound of two-sided 90% CI < 10 ms)
qtc_thresholds.positive_tqt_definition: Upper bound of one-sided 95% CI >= 10 ms
qtc_thresholds.discontinuation_thresholds: QT/QTc > 500 ms or change > 60 ms from baseline

tqt_study_requirements.parallel_design_when: Long half-life, carryover effects, multiple groups
tqt_study_requirements.dose: Supratherapeutic (commonly 2x high clinical exposure at mean Cmax)
tqt_study_requirements.assay_sensitivity: Lower bound of one-sided 95% CI of positive control effect must be > 0 ms
tqt_study_requirements.ecg_method: 12-lead standard; replicate ECGs (~3) at each time point; blinded reading by few skilled readers
tqt_study_requirements.preferred_correction: Fridericia (QTc = QT/RR^0.33); Bazett no longer warranted as primary
tqt_study_requirements.baseline: Time-matched for parallel, pre-dose for crossover

c_qtc_analysis.methodology: Concentration-QTc modeling using all available dose data
c_qtc_analysis.negative_criterion: Upper bound of two-sided 90% CI for QTc effect < 10 ms at highest clinically relevant exposure
c_qtc_analysis.data_sources: Can come from FIH, MAD, or other studies — not limited to dedicated QT study
c_qtc_analysis.models: Linear, Emax; must be pre-specified; test for hysteresis
c_qtc_analysis.positive_control_waiver: Not needed if data characterize response at sufficient multiple (commonly 2x) of high clinical exposure

conditions: Concentrations well above max therapeutic dose at steady state
Wide dose range explored in early phase
Positive control included or waived per conditions above
hERG assay + in vivo QT assay following best practice show low risk
Alternative QT study with as many TQT design features as possible
Totality of evidence argument at marketing application

tqt_waiver_conditions.large_proteins_mabs: TQT not necessary unless proarrhythmic risk suggested by data
tqt_waiver_conditions.combination_products: TQT unnecessary if components individually lack relevant QT effects

low_tdp_risk_criteria: hERG safety margin higher than threshold defined from reference drugs with known TdP risk under same protocol
No QTc prolongation in in vivo assay at exposures exceeding clinical exposures of parent + major metabolites
Clinical ECG data: upper bound of 90% CI for delta-QTc < 10 ms with no outlier imbalances
Cardiovascular safety database shows no increased rate of proarrhythmic AEs

herg_safety_margin_calculation.denominator: Free drug high clinical exposure (Cmax,ss under worst-case intrinsic/extrinsic factor)
herg_safety_margin_calculation.unbound_fraction_floor: If experimentally < 1%, set to 1% for calculation
herg_safety_margin_calculation.comparison: Compared to safety margins for reference drugs with known TdP risk under same protocol

follow_up_studies: Additional ion channel currents (IKs, INaL, ICaL)
In vivo telemetry studies with exposure-response modeling
Qualified proarrhythmia risk prediction models (in silico)

mandatory_data_always_collected: Adverse events leading to study drug discontinuation
Pregnancy and lactation exposure and outcomes
Adverse events of special interest (AESIs) identified in protocol
Baseline data (demographics, disease characteristics, concurrent illnesses, concomitant therapies)
criteria_supporting_selective_collection: Thorough understanding of mechanism of action including off-target effects
Knowledge of safety profile of same pharmacologic class
Number of drug-exposed subjects sufficiently exceeds ICH E1 requirements
Consistency of safety profile across previous trials
Intensity of safety monitoring in previous trials was adequate
Planned dose does not exceed previously studied dose
Duration of prior exposure is adequate
Comparable drug product (form, substance, excipients, route)
Similar population (demographics, concurrent illnesses, concomitant therapies, CYP phenotype)
Drug metabolism and excretion well described
Adequate quantity and quality of post-approval safety data
implementation_approaches: Selective for all patients throughout the trial
Comprehensive for specific subsets, selective for others
Comprehensive for initial period, selective thereafter
Comprehensive for a representative subset of the population
selective_safety_data_collection_e19.regulatory_requirement: Prospective agreement with all regulatory authorities required

key_requirements: Adaptive designs must not lower scientific and regulatory standards for safety
Stopping for efficacy requires sufficient information to evaluate safety and benefit-risk
Number and timing of interim analyses must be specified and justified
Type I error probability must be appropriately controlled
Assessment must consider heterogeneity between stage-wise results as potential safety signal
Independent DMC should review unblinded interim data
Participants, investigators, and sponsor should be blinded to treatment assignments and accumulating summary data

evidence_needed: Novelty of product (availability of data from similar molecules)
monitoring_requirements: Infant follow-up duration on case-by-case basis
Account for delayed diagnosis of birth defects and neurodevelopmental disorders
Ongoing post-marketing safety monitoring for rare or delayed outcomes
breastfeeding_considerations: Extent of absorption by the child
Potential benefits and risks for patient and child

risk_characterization.scope: Each important identified and potential risk

elements: Weighing characterized benefits against characterized risks per approved indication
integrated_analysis.conclusion_required: Clear statement on whether benefit-risk balance remains favourable

dsur_methodology.approach: Succinct statement on perceived balance between cumulative safety data and anticipated efficacy/benefits; note changes since previous DSUR. Not intended as full benefit-risk assessment.

patient_preference_e22.role: PPS can provide measures of risk thresholds that inform benefit-risk assessment
patient_preference_e22.application: Acceptability of benefit-risk trade-offs; development of risk-mitigation strategies and risk management plans

real_world_evidence_safety_e23.framework: E23 covers RWE for effectiveness, complementing ICH M14 for safety
real_world_evidence_safety_e23.note: Together M14 (safety) and E23 (effectiveness) form comprehensive framework for RWD/RWE in regulatory decision-making across full benefit-risk spectrum
